title: DC-19 "Stealth" carbine
content:

As the DC-19 "Stealth" [[Blaster carbine/Legends|carbine]] is a [[Rifle/Legends|rifle]] with normal accuracy, according to ''[[Echoes of the Jedi]]'', it has an optimum range from 0 to 30 squares and a maximum range of 300 squares, according to the ''Saga Edition Core Rulebook''. The '''DC-19 "Stealth" carbine''' was a [[Blaster carbine/Legends|blaster carbine]] in [[BlasTech Industries/Legends|BlasTech Industries]]' [[DC-15]] [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] line. The DC-19 was equipped with a sound suppressor unit for silent operations and an optional stealth function, which used a refined [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] gas mixture that made the DC-19's plasma bolts invisible to the naked [[Eye/Legends|eye]]. However, the tibanna mixture was highly expensive, had to be reloaded after ten shots, and needed to be cooled down after each shot to prevent damage to the carbine's dampeners. The DC-19 was used almost exclusively by [[Clone shadow trooper/Legends|clone shadow troopers]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and for a short [[Time/Legends|time]] afterward, when the [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] shadow [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] became part of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]].

==Description==
Part of [[BlasTech Industries/Legends|BlasTech Industries]]'<ref name="Weapons" /> [[DC-15]] [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] line,<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|I|175|DC-19 stealth carbine}}</ref> the DC-19 "Stealth" [[Blaster carbine/Legends|carbine]] was a medium-sized blaster carbine<ref name="shadowtroopers" /> that weighed five [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] and was worth 3,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Although the DC-19 had no [[Stun/Legends|stun]] setting,<ref name="Echoes of the Jedi" /> it did have a number of unique features, including a sound suppressor unit that allowed the user to operate the blaster in virtually complete silence. The DC-19 also had an alternate stealth firing mode that used a refined mixture of [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] gas, making the bolt invisible to the naked [[Eye/Legends|eye]], a great boon to [[Sniper/Legends|snipers]].<ref name="shadowtroopers" />

However, the invisible tibanna mix had to be reloaded after every ten shots, and there was a required cool down [[Time/Legends|time]] between each individual shot to protect the blaster's dampeners from overheating.<ref name="shadowtroopers" /> Similar technology was later used in the [[Nightstinger sporting blaster rifle]], utilized by [[Imperial commando/Legends|Imperial commandos]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Specter">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref> The carbine had an optimum firing range of up to forty-five [[Meter/Legends|meters]] and a maximum range of 450 meters.<ref name="Range" />

==History==
[[File:Utapaushadowtrooper.jpg|thumb|left|175px|A clone shadow trooper]]
The DC-19 was used extensively by the elite [[Clone shadow trooper/Legends|clone shadow troopers]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Unlike most [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]], who generally used the standard [[DC-15S/Legends|DC-15S]] carbine, the shadow [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] used the sound suppressor unit of the "Stealth" carbine for their covert operations deep inside Confederate territory. Shadow trooper snipers also used the invisible [[Laser/Legends|laser]] bolt in the alternate tibanna gas firing mode to great effect.<ref name="shadowtroopers" />

In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="Sith">[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]]</ref> after the end of the Clone Wars, the Republic's reformation into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] being declared enemies of the state, shadow troopers were absorbed into the new [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]], still using the DC-19 during the early part of the Empire's reign.<ref name="Echoes of the Jedi" /> Circa [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Echoes of the Jedi]]'' states that the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vhiin Thorla]] traveled to the [[Almas Academy]] two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the declaration of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. This thus indicates that ''Echoes of the Jedi'' is set in circa [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]].</ref> Imperial [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] and [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Valin Draco]] took four shadow troopers—equipped with DC-19 carbines—on an expedition to the destroyed [[Almas]] [[Almas Academy|Jedi Academy]], investigating a powerful [[Vergence/Legends|Force nexus]] that had appeared on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Draco and his shadow troopers eventually made their way down into the Academy's sublevels, where the Inquisitor discovered an ancient [[Darth Rivan's holocron|Sith holocron]].<ref name="Echoes of the Jedi" />

However, as Draco began to examine the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]], the group encountered several members of an [[Alderaanian Resistance|armed resistance movement]] working for the fugitive [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Denia]]. The shadow troopers attempted to ambush the resistance members, but the rebels engaged the Imperial [[Troop/Legends|troops]] in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|melee combat]] and defeated them. With the shadow troopers dispatched, a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] resistance member took one of the [[Cloning/Legends|clone's]] DC-19 carbines for the rebels' own use.<ref name="Echoes of the Jedi" />

==Behind the scenes==
The DC-19 "Stealth" carbine was created in [[2007]] by [[Sam D. Garito]], writing under the alias of "Maso Tirag," in his winning entry for the clone shadow trooper in [[StarWars.com]]'s [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] contest, ''[[What's The Story?]]'' The DC-19 carbine then appeared, along with clone shadow troopers, in the fourth adventure of the ''[[Dawn of Defiance]]'' campaign for the [[Saga Edition|''Star Wars Roleplaying Game, Saga Edition'']], ''[[Echoes of the Jedi]]'', published in [[April]] of [[2008]].
